title: Modeling CAR-T cell therapy on-chip
summary: Researchers have developed an organ-on-chip platform that models CAR-T cell therapy interactions with vascularized tumors, enabling more predictive preclinical testing of cancer immunotherapies. The microphysiological system uses tumor-on-chip technology to recapitulate the tumor microenvironment and evaluate CAR-T cell efficacy in a controlled, human-relevant context.

title: Broad-spectrum Ebola vaccine
summary: A new pan-Ebola vaccine candidate has been reported that provides broad-spectrum protection across multiple Ebola virus species, representing a significant advance over strain-specific approaches. This development could enable more robust pandemic preparedness against emergent filovirus threats and simplify response strategies for outbreak containment.

title: Next-gen cryo promises transplant surgeons more time
summary: Sub-zero cryopreservation technologies that reduce ice damage in donor organs are extending the shelf life of transplanted tissues, giving transplant teams more flexibility in patient matching and logistics. This advancement could significantly reduce organ wastage and improve outcomes by broadening the geographic and temporal window for successful transplant procedures.
